name: TAT2
summary: TAT2 is a telomerase-activating small molecule normalized for ex vivo human CD8+ T-cell antiviral-function evidence.
mechanism_summary: Activates telomerase in immune cells to test whether telomere biology can improve T-cell function ex vivo.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: TAT2 enhanced antiviral function in human CD8+ T cells ex vivo; TAT2 treatment of human CD8+ T cells ex vivo.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
